displayTitle: Lift-Off and Frequency: The Two Knobs You Touch Most
kicker: ET LEVEL I // LIFT-OFF & FREQUENCY LAB

beats.narration: Two variables decide whether a surface scan tells the truth: the coil-to-metal gap, and the test frequency. This lab puts both on the bench.

payload.title: LIFT-OFF & FREQUENCY
payload.sub: One knob you turn, one gap your wrist controls.

beats.narration: First, lift-off. Set an EDM notch to eighty percent height, then slide shims under the probe. The signal collapses, steepest in the first fraction of a millimetre.

payload.heading: Shim the probe, starve the notch

beats.narration: Fight back two ways. Rotate the display so lift-off runs horizontal and cracks break vertical, then steady the probe with holders and constant light pressure.

payload.heading: Park lift-off flat, let cracks break up
payload.note: ROTATE LIFT-OFF HORIZONTAL · CRACK BREAKS VERTICAL

beats.narration: Second, frequency. Sweeping higher widens the angle between lift-off and the flaw. Drop to ten kilohertz and a deeper, second-layer flaw appears.
Surface work runs high, penetration runs low, and the depth you care about must stay inside three standard depths. That is the trade.

payload.sub: Control spacing, pick frequency, keep the flaw in reach.
